nyheter.title: New executive order tightens defense supply chain waiver rules
nyheter.summary: A new executive order aims to make it harder for U.S. defense contractors to buy critical minerals and other materials from prohibited foreign suppliers.

nyheter.summary: The new organization that will oversee surface shipbuilding for the U.S. Navy and one Coast Guard program has reached its initial operational capability overseeing major surface ship and aircraft carrier construction, according to a July 16 memo obtained by USNI News. nyheter.summary: The Italian prime minister said the deal is a “recognition of Italian defense industry but also of how Europe can reinforce its autonomy by investing in its own technological capacity and companies.”

nyheter.summary: MANILA, PHILIPPINES – A Philippine sailor was injured today at Second Thomas Shoal following a clash with a Chinese small boat at the contested South China Sea maritime feature. The melee resulted in a blow to the head of a Philippine service member, according to photos and video released from the incident by the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP). Dubbed the “Golden Defender,” the ship is based on the National Security Multi-Mission Vessel design that Hanwha is finishing for the U.S. Maritime Administration’s training ships. nyheter.summary: The U.S. Navy’s expensive effort to install hypersonic missiles on three Zumwalt-class destroyers is two years behind schedule.

nyheter.title: Spanish Navy to Lead NATO’s Atlantic Task Force Until June 2027
nyheter.summary: The Spanish Navy has assumed command of NATO’s naval force dedicated to patrolling the North Atlantic and will lead the force for the next year, NATO announced Tuesday. From July of 2026 to June of 2027, NATO’s Commander Task Force Atlantic (CTF-AT) will be led by five Spanish ships, the Spanish Marine Corps landing force and eight ships from allied nations. nyheter.title: USS Fort Lauderdale Returns from 11-month Deployment to the Caribbean
nyheter.summary: Amphibious transport dock USS Fort Lauderdale (LPD-28) pulled into Naval Station Norfolk on Thursday, capping off a nearly year-long deployment in the Caribbean, the Navy announced. The ship originally deployed as part of the Iwo Jima Amphibious Ready Group and supported missions ranging from the early January raid in Venezuela that captured the country’s former president, Nicolás Maduro, to multiple humanitarian aid and disaster relief response efforts in the region. “During its 334-day deploym

nyheter.title: Coast Guard Ends Middle East Mission, Moves Cutter Squadron to Western Pacific
nyheter.summary: The U.S. Coast Guard is transitioning its Patrol Forces Southwest Asia (PATFORSWA) to the Western Pacific, ending a more than 20-year forward-deployed mission in the Middle East. The Coast Guard’s Expeditionary Cutter Squadron that makes up PATFORSWA, which was previously forward deployed to Bahrain, will instead operate from Guam and the Pacific Islands, the service announced Wednesday. nyheter.title: Blue Water Autonomy, Saildrone Sue Navy Over MUSV Rejection
nyheter.summary: Two companies that were passed over for the U.S. Navy’s Medium Unmanned Surface Vessel program are taking the service to court over the decision, USNI News has learned. Saildrone and Blue Water Autonomy filed suit in late June in the United States Court of Federal Claims in Washington, D.C., with two separate complaints alleging the Navy didn’t follow its own rules in how it selected contenders for the first generation of larger autonomous vessels. In May, the Navy tapped seven out of about two

nyheter.title: Cahill Takes Command of U.S. Naval Surface Force
nyheter.summary: Vice Adm. Joe Cahill took over as the Navy’s top surface warfare officer earlier this month, USNI News has learned. Cahill, who most recently was the commander of Naval Surface Force Atlantic, took the helm of U.S. Naval Surface Force on July 3, according to a service news release. Cahill succeeds Vice Adm. Brendan McLane, who is retiring after serving as the SWOBOSS since December 2023. The ceremony occurred over the Fourth of July holiday weekend at Naval Amphibious Base Coronado in California

nyheter.title: Taiwan Mobilizes HIMARS, Anti-ship Missiles For Major Defense Drill
nyheter.summary: Taiwan’s most capable missile launchers are being deployed across the island for a five-day defense drill designed to enhance joint operations and develop concepts for targeting enemy aircraft and vessels. nyheter.title: MARAD Ship Suffers Fire in Baltimore
nyheter.summary: Two lifeboats aboard U.S. Maritime Administration’s Charles L. Gilliland caught fire Monday in the Port of Baltimore. Baltimore City Fire Department responded around 11 a.m. to the two-alarm fire, meaning additional resources were needed to control the flames. There were no injuries reported. The cause of the fire is under investigation. Gilliland is one of MARAD’s roll-on, roll-off logistics ships.
